title: Complement-fixation by antibodies to Plasmodium falciparum
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"o5nvo","text":"In malaria, the fixation and activation of serum complement proteins by antibodies can support immunity by mediating parasite lysis, inhibiting host cell motility and invasion and promoting phagocytosis. Antibodies, bound to malaria antigens, may bind to C1q via their Fc region, initiating the classical complement cascade; C1q is the first step in the classical activation pathway. This protocol measures C1q-fixation by antibodies that target whole Plasmodium falciparum parasites, which can include merozoites, sporozoites, or gametocytes/gametes. The procedure is similar to an ELISA and has the advantage of having high reproducibility, good quantification, and is amenable to high throughput.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"kiyi7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

(PBS)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d3r5m","text":"PBS-Tween 0.05% (1X PBS with 0.05% Tween milk-PBS","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"72ibk","text":"5% skim milk-PBS","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dsvtu","text":"Purified human C1q (e.g. Complement C1q, Human (Millipore #204876))","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9uumu","text":"Rabbit anti-C1q antibody (e.g. Beeson lab in-house reagent OR C1q anti-rabbit IgG (e.g. Goat Anti-Rabbit IgG Antibody (Millipore #AP307P))","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ea4di","text":"Substrate (TMB (Life technologies #00-2023)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7h1m7","text":"Sulphuric acid (H2SO4) steps: Coat 96-well flat bottom plate with 50 μL/well freshly isolated whole merozoites (or sporozoites, or gametocytes/gametes) diluted in 1X PBS at 1 x 10^6 parasites/mL (50 000 merozoites/well) and incubate at 37˚C for 2 hours.
Block plate using 10% skim milk-PBS (200 μL/well) for 2 hours at 37˚C.
Wash plate three times using PBS-Tween 0.05%.
Add 50 μL/well serum samples, diluted 1/200 in 5% skim milk-PBS, in duplicate and incubate for 2 hours at room temperature (RT).
Wash plate three times using PBS-Tween 0.05%.
Dilute purified human C1q to 10 μg/mL in 5% skim milk-PBS. Add 30 μL to each well and incubate for 30 mins at RT.
Wash plate three times using PBS-Tween 0.05%.
Dilute rabbit anti-C1q antibody at 1/2000 in 5% skim milk-PBS and add 50 μL/well. Incubate for 1 hour at RT.
Wash plate three times using PBS-Tween 0.05%.
Dilute goat anti-rabbit IgG HRP at 1/2000 in 5% skim milk-PBS and add 50 μL/well. Incubate HRP-conjugated antibody for 1 hour at RT.
Wash plate three times using PBS-Tween 0.05%.
Add 50 μL of TMB substrate into each well and incubate in the dark for 1 hour at RT. (Substrate works best at RT, remove from fridge 1 hour prior to use.)
Stop the reaction using 1M Sulphuric acid (50 μL/well).
Measure absorbance at 450 nm for TMB.

The dilution of serum samples should be optimised for each cohort. Positive serum controls and negative controls (such as malaria-naïve serum and no-serum control wells) should be included on each plate to correct for background and inter-assay variation. We use our own in-house anti-C1q antibody. If using a C1q antibody from a commercial supplier, it will require work-up assays to ensure specificity and sensitivity. Coating of wells with whole parasites will need to be optimised depending on the source of cells.
